description:
Trigger: user provisions a P-384 or P-521 ECDSA key on their HSM (common in FIPS environments where P-256 may not be the default).

Maintenance consequence: detect_ec_algorithm at lib.rs:159-193 hardcodes P-256 OID and returns SecretError::Backend with 'unsupported EC curve' for anything else. README does not state 'P-256 only'. The user assumes any EC curve works because (a) PKCS#11 supports them all, (b) the README says 'PKCS#11 v2.40-compliant'.

This is the second of a pair (Ed25519 sibling) and produces the same class of issue reports. Fix: extend detect_ec_algorithm to recognize P-384 (OID 1.3.132.0.34) and P-521 (1.3.132.0.35), add SigningAlgorithm::EcdsaP384Sha384 and EcdsaP521Sha512 variants to secretx-core::SigningAlgorithm (already #[non_exhaustive] so this is non-breaking), and add corresponding sign match arms with Mechanism::EcdsaSha384 / EcdsaSha512.

If the design choice is intentionally to support only P-256 for now, document it explicitly in README under a 'Supported algorithms' section, and emit an error message that says so directly: 'PKCS#11 backend supports only EC P-256 in this version; see <url> for tracking issue'.
title: Only P-256 supported among EC curves; P-384/P-521 silently rejected — undocumented limitation
